colored_zones.name: Up Zone
colored_zones.description: Area between Base line and Upper deviation line

colored_zones.name: Down Zone
colored_zones.description: Area between Base line and Lower deviation line

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: Two distinct colored zones (Up zone above Base, Down zone below Base)
Up zone: Blue/Purple (#2196F3) with 15% opacity
Down zone: Beige/Yellow (#FFE0B2) with 15% opacity
Tooltip has exactly 7 buttons (not 8)
Base line is dashed (#FF9800), Up/Down lines are solid (#2196F3)

trend_line.name: Trend Line
trend_line.description: Dashed line connecting P1 and P2

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: Background fills must be distinct per level interval.
CRITICAL: 8 buttons in Toolbar.
Labels have 2 boolean toggles: Prices AND Levels.
Log Scale support required.
Mathematical Model: Parallel lines projected from Base Line (P1-P2) based on height unit (P3).
Strict parallelism required (no rotation of individual levels).
Inversion support (negative height) must be seamless.

lines.name: Connection Line (Optional)
lines.description: Usually forms a channel or wedge

implementation_notes: Distinct buttons for Border Color and Fill Color in tooltip
No explicit Inputs/Coordinates tabs observed in this version
Includes a 'Text' tab in settings for labels
Alert button present in tooltip

lines.name: Offset Line
lines.description: Defined by 3rd click and independent handles

colored_zones.name: Channel Fill
colored_zones.description: Area between the two lines. Supports Bowtie crossing.

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: 3-click creation (Line 1 -> Height).
CRITICAL: 4 handles are independent after creation, allowing non-parallel and Bowtie shapes.
Tooltip has exactly 11 buttons.
No Inputs or Coordinates tabs present in this tool.

median_line.name: Median
median_line.description: Origin P1 to midpoint of P2-P3

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: Median must pass through geometric midpoint of P2 and P3.
Modified Schiff geometry: Origin = midpoint(P1, P2).
Levels are managed as individual parallel offsets from the median slope.

median_line.name: Median
median_line.description: Origin = Midpoint(P1, P2). Passing through Midpoint(P2, P3).

trend_line.name: Trend Line
trend_line.description: Solid line connecting P1 to P2.

implementation_notes: Origin of the fork is strictly the midpoint of P1 and P2.
A solid trend line must remain visible between P1 and P2.
Tooltip must show exactly 10 buttons (including Move handle).
Geometric calculations must support Bowtie (crossing P3 beyond P2).

median_line.name: Median
median_line.description: Origin = Midpoint(P1, P2) - both axis shifted. Passing through Midpoint(P2, P3).

trend_line.name: Anchor Line
trend_line.description: Solid line connecting P1 to P2. Visible when selected.

implementation_notes: Origin of the fork is precisely the midpoint (geometric center) of P1 and P2.
Geometric logic must support Bowtie (crossing P3 beyond P2).
Tooltip must show exactly 9 buttons (Visibility icon is hidden for this tool).
Force 'modified_schiff' style by default when selected from this specific menu item.

median_line.name: Median
median_line.description: Origin P1 to midpoint of P2-P3. P1 is geometrically located at the midpoint of actual user clicks A and B.

implementation_notes: Origin of the fork is the midpoint of P1 and P2.
Similar to Modified Schiff in geometry, but often treated as a distinct tool.
Tooltip: 8 buttons (Matches standard Pitchfork, note: earlier audit said 8 for Inside/Standard).
Force 'inside' style by default.
Calculation: Extension = PriceC + (Ratio * (PriceB - PriceA)).
Geometric Torture: Tool becomes Bowtie when C > B, remains mathematically valid.
Tooltip buttons: 8 total (excluding separators).
Geometry is strictly vertical.
Trend Line is the guide between P1 and P2.
Labels have 9 position combinations.
CRITICAL: 2 clicks required (P1 origin, P2 bounding box pilot).
Radial lines must converge at P1 and end at the edges of the P1-P2 rectangle based on ratios.
Torture: Handles must allow full inversion (flipped扇子).
Projection: T = T_C + R * (T_B - T_A)
Trend lines A-B and B-C are vital for visual context.
Labels anchor to the bottom/top of the grid regardless of price.
Crucial: 3 clicks required for activation loop.
Concentric circles radiate from P1.
Radius of Level 1.0 is the distance P1-P2.
Trend Line (P1-P2) acts as the label axis.
Critique: Geometry is 3-point. Arcs are ellipses. Construction lines (P1-P2, P2-P3) are dashed.
Critique: Labels should be placed along the arcs.
Critique: 'Full Circles' option changes arcs to complete ellipses.
STRICTLY BOUNDED: Side rays strictly terminate at the maximum enabled Fibonacci level (default 1.0).
RADIUS INTERPOLATION: Arcs morph between Ray1 and Ray2 lengths to connect handles perfectly.
HIT-TEST: Sector-based hit detection enables professional selection and whole-shape dragging.
FIDELITY: Labels positioned inside arcs near Side Ray 1 for maximum clarity (matches Image 4).

tools.description: Pitchfan — Angular fan radiating from P1 pivot through angularly-interpolated lines between P2 and P3 direction handles.

visual_elements.fills: Triangular fill zones between adjacent enabled lines (optional, fillEnabled toggle)

implementation_notes: TYPE: FibonacciToolType (not DrawingToolType) — confirmed 2026-02-19.
CATEGORY: TOOL_CATEGORIES.FIBONACCI — shows in Fibonacci dropdown in sidebar.
HIT-TEST: Inherited from FIB_PURE_TOOLS block in hitTest, check lines radiating from P1.
FILL: Triangular sector fills between each pair of adjacent fan lines using ctx.moveTo(P1)+lineTo(eA)+lineTo(eB)+closePath.
CONSTRUCTION: During creation (pts.length<3), shows dashed purple lines from P1 to P2 and P3 handles.
MEDIAN COLOR: drawing.style.color overrides the default red (#f44336) for the median (t=0.5) line only.
2-click interaction.
Internal grid of 7 Price and 7 Time levels.
Support for Angles (diagonal Bowtie effect) and Reverse logic.

visual_elements.description: Rectangular grid with 6 symmetric levels (0-5), fan lines from P1 origin, elliptical arcs from P1, and uniform background fill.
visual_elements.geometry: Flexible rectangle (any aspect ratio). P1=bottom-left anchor, P2=top-right corner. Internal elements scale to bounding box. NOT forced square despite name.

torture_test.vertical_inversion: Stable rectangle, fans/arcs adapt, no bowtie
torture_test.horizontal_inversion: Stable rectangle, fans/arcs adapt, no bowtie

implementation_notes: 2-click interaction: P1=anchor (origin of fans/arcs), P2=opposite corner.
Grid: 6 equally-spaced horizontal+vertical lines at ratios 0/5, 1/5, 2/5, 3/5, 4/5, 5/5 of bounding box.
Fans: Lines from P1, target point on far edge computed by Gann ratio. Fan R:T means slope (R*H)/(T*W) in canvas coords.
Arcs: Elliptical, centered at P1. For ratio Rx:Ry, radii = (Rx/5)*W, (Ry/5)*H. Clipped to bounding box.
"Fixed" means the Gann fan angles are fixed relative to the bounding box (not to price/time scale).
Background: single uniform fill covering entire bounding box.
Reverse: swaps P1/P2 anchor roles (fans/arcs radiate from P2 instead of P1).

interaction.notes: Click A = Anchor/Origin. Click B = Direction of 1/1 line slope. All 9 fan lines radiate from Anchor A.

geometry.anchor: Point A = Origin of all fan lines
geometry.direction: Point B = defines slope of the 1/1 line (price_delta / bar_delta)
geometry.extension: All lines extend infinitely past Point B (clipped to visible chart area)
geometry.slope_formula_log: log_price(t) = log(p1) + (log_slope * ratio_num/ratio_den) * (t - t1); then exp(log_price)
geometry.time_axis: bar_index (not timestamp) to avoid weekend gaps
geometry.reverse_behavior: When reversed, slope direction inverts (bearish fan from top-left anchor)

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: Use bar_index for time axis, NOT timestamp — avoids weekend gap distortion
CRITICAL: For Log scale — calculate slope in log(price) space, then exp() back for display
Line 1/1 is the 45-degree reference (balanced price-time). It is the anchor for all other ratios.
Lines ABOVE 1/1 (2/1, 3/1, 4/1, 8/1) = steep bullish angles (1 bar for N price units)
Lines BELOW 1/1 (1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/8) = shallow bullish angles (N bars for 1 price unit)
Fill zones: color spans between two adjacent lines as a solid trapezoid fill
Clipping: All lines extend infinitely, clipped only by the canvas/chart visible bounds
Reverse: Inverts the slope sign, turning a bullish fan into a bearish fan from the same anchor
Handle 2 (on 1/1 line) can be dragged to re-slope the base reference line

sequence: Click A (start point - typically a swing high or low)
Click B (opposite swing - AB segment fixed, BC preview begins)
Click C (retracement of AB - BC segment fixed, CD preview begins, AC diagonal with ratio shown)
Click D (extension of BC - pattern complete, BD diagonal with ratio shown)

visual_elements.note_no_fill: Unlike XABCD/Cypher, ABCD pattern has NO fill zones between points

labels.name: A, B, C, D
labels.note: Labels alternate above/below based on zigzag direction (bullish W vs bearish M)

torture_geometry.inversion: Fully dynamic - if A is below B creates bullish W-shape, if A is above B creates bearish M-shape
torture_geometry.stretch: AC and BD diagonals recalculate ratios in real-time during handle drag
torture_geometry.overcross: Reconfigures segments without breaking - no rigid bowtie behavior
torture_geometry.note: NOT a rigid trapeze - fully flexible zigzag geometry

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: 4 clicks only (A, B, C, D) — NOT 5 like XABCD/Cypher
CRITICAL: NO fill zones (fillEnabled: false) — key difference from XABCD
Default color is #089981 (TradingView teal) — NOT #2962FF blue
AC diagonal shows BC/AB ratio (harmonic retracement label)
BD diagonal shows CD/BC ratio (harmonic extension label)
Labels are CIRCLES (not rectangles) — key visual difference from XABCD
Smart label placement: alternates above/below based on zigzag direction
Pattern is purely a connected path A→B→C→D with 2 internal diagonals

sequence: Click B (second point, fill preview begins)
Click C (third point, triangular fill visible A-B-C)

torture_geometry.inversion: Fully dynamic. Allows points to be freely placed.
torture_geometry.stretch: Pattern handles stretch the polygon arbitrarily.
torture_geometry.overcross: Bowtie effect. When AB crosses CD, the single fill polygon creates two adjoining triangles meeting at intersection, confirming true fill path A-B-C-D-A canvas behavior.
torture_geometry.note: A highly dynamic pattern, not locked to a strict convex shape.

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: Bowtie geometric fill verified via subagent.
4 structural points (A, B, C, D).
Solid path AB-BC-CD, dashed diagonals/closures AD, AC, BD.
Fill works by tracing a simple path through all 4 points, canvas natural intersecting fill applies.
7 structural points.
Labels start from P3 (1) to P7 (3).
3 distinct filled triangles: [P1,P2,P3], [P3,P4,P5], [P5,P6,P7].
Geometric measurements (ratios) appear on dashed lines.

geometry.description: Infinite sine wave based on crossing point (anchor) and first peak (amplitude/period).
